question_text: A location in Antarctica is at 70°S latitude. On the summer solstice (21 December), calculate the angle of the Sun above the horizon at solar noon. Show your working.
correct_answer: The Sun is directly above 23.5°S on the summer solstice. The angle = 90° - (70° - 23.5°) = 90° - 46.5° = 43.5° above the horizon.
explanation: On the summer solstice in the Southern Hemisphere, the Sun is at its most northerly point (directly above 23.5°S). From 70°S, you need to calculate the angular distance and subtract from 90°. The answer is 43.5°.
